status_plain_language: Echo Guardian v0.2 has passed the production-foundation release gate for local-first safety monitoring architecture. It is not certified for medical, HIPAA, GDPR, emergency-response, or clinical use.

production_laws.description: Nothing hidden; every monitoring, policy, export, and escalation state must be visible and explainable.
The core safety loop must not depend on cloud services.
Bathroom and bedroom monitoring are blocked by default in v0.2.
Raw sensor samples must not cross the ingestion boundary or be retained by default.
Meaningful state changes are written to the append-only audit log.
Exports require explicit user request and automatic export is denied.
Live escalation requires authorized contacts, test-alert readiness, and minimal disclosure.
Active monitoring is blocked unless required health and policy gates pass.
Audit tampering, reordering, and truncation are detectable through verification and checkpoint comparison.

known_limitations.summary: Not certified as HIPAA compliant, GDPR compliant, medical-device compliant, clinical, or emergency-response system.
Real mobile OS sensor adapters and Secure Enclave bindings remain implementation targets beyond the Python foundation package.
Bathroom and bedroom monitoring are intentionally blocked in v0.2.
Emergency-services routing is disabled by default and not implemented as a production path in v0.2.
ML-KEM and ML-DSA-87 are specified in the cryptographic profile but require vetted production library integration.
No clinical validation or safety efficacy claim has been established.

open_risks.mitigation: Collect real-world validation data under consent and tune conservative thresholds.
Add sensor diversity, failure injection, missed-event review, and incident analysis.
Design native iOS/Android service models compatible with OS background restrictions.
Implement platform-backed keys, rotation, recovery boundaries, and migration audit events.
Map jurisdiction requirements and limit claims to reviewed evidence.
Perform usability testing with elderly users and users with vision, speech, hearing, motor, and cognitive limitations.

next_release_requirements_v0_3: Native platform sensor adapter implementation plan for iOS and Android.
Secure Enclave / Keychain / Keystore integration design and proof path.
Real provider adapter boundary for live contact delivery with test-alert verification.
Expanded security hardening suite with explicit tests/security directory.
Privacy rights workflow implementation and evidence artifacts.
Accessibility screen-flow prototype and user-facing copy review.
CI pipeline with test, lint, schema validation, audit verification, and artifact hashing.
